title: Black Curs
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Otheruses|Black}}
{{Organization
|image=[[File:OhNoMoreSunglasses.jpg]]
|name=Black Curs

{{Quote|Sarge? You ever think about becoming a mercenary?|Dirk Harkness to Jai Raventhorn|<ref name="Gathering">*{{StoryCite|story=Gathering Shadows|book=Tales from the New Republic}}</ref>}}
The '''Black Curs''' were a band of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] formed [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Though they functioned as an independent group, the Curs were affiliated with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], devoted to scouting and intelligence operations to undermine the crumbling [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. [[Spacer/Legends|Spacers]] in the Black Curs included [[Dirk Harkness]], [[Platt Okeefe/Legends|Platt Okeefe]], [[Tru'eb Cholakk]], [[Jai Raventhorn]], and [[Starter]]: former [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], outlaws, and Republic personnel impatient with military life. All had personal reasons for hating the Empire.

==Description==
While Dirk Harkness founded the Black Curs and was considered their leader, the band always had a loose and informal organization. The members related to each other as friends as comrades rather than superiors and subordinates.<ref name="Chessa" /> Besides the core members, the Curs recruited others, mostly orphaned young people, to man their base; some of these recruits brought younger siblings to live there with them.<ref name="Blues">{{JournalCite|8|Black Curs Blues}}</ref> 

[[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] considered the Curs to be a friendly mercenary group and not an official branch.<ref name="Chessa" /> [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] oversaw the assignment of missions to the Curs, as he did with other off-the-record mercenary teams.<ref name="Spin">{{SW|url=news/the-star-wars-spy-game-spin-declassified|text=The ''Star Wars'' Spy Game: SPIN Declassified|int=The Star Wars Spy Game: SPIN Declassified|nolive=1}}</ref> Some leaders in the [[New Republic Provisional Council|Provisional Council]], including [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] and [[Borsk Fey'lya]], opposed this use of mercenaries to conduct intelligence, but Cracken continued to make use of them.<ref name="Blues" />

The Black Curs engaged in small-scale covert operations to obtain tactical information about Imperial forces,<ref name="Blues" /> assess the political climate on Imperial worlds,<ref name="Rogue" /> and facilitate contact between New Republic operatives so that their intelligence could be shared.<ref name="Blues" /> The location of the team's base of operations, [[Black Curs Base]], was kept secret from the mercenaries' enemies and allies alike.<ref name="Rogue" />

==History==
===Gathering shadowy mercenaries===
The outlaws who formed the Black Curs were old friends and associates.<ref name="Chessa" /> With the Empire starting to crumble, they felt driven to work with the [[Alliance of Free Planets]] (soon to become the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]) to get revenge on the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] for its crimes and atrocities that had caused each of them to suffer in different ways.<ref name="Blues" /> Dirk Harkness briefly enlisted in New Republic Intelligence and was commissioned as a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], but he quickly realized that this life was not for him and resigned after a few weeks. Instead, he gathered like-minded spacers to work closely with NRI while remaining independent.<ref name="Chessa" /> The mercenary band provided the Republic with recon information on Imperial actions with the hope that the information would lead to the Empire's destruction.<ref name="Blues" />

===Recon and Report===
For the next six months, the Black Curs explored the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|space lanes]] between the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] and the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], gathering information on the state of the Empire after Palpatine's death and looking for routes that the Rebellion could take in pursuit of its ultimate goal, the capture of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. This work took the Curs to [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]], [[Kelada/Legends|Kelada]], the [[Sluis sector/Legends|Sluis sector]], [[Salliche/Legends|Salliche]], [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]], the [[Boeus sector/Legends|Boeus sector]], [[Kaal/Legends|Kaal]], and elsewhere. They explored the status of the emerging [[Warlord/Legends|Imperial Warlords]] and investigated the [[Free Systems]] abandoned by any Imperial regime. Harkness compiled an extensive report, titled "[[Recon & Report: The Journey to Coruscant]]," that summarized the team's findings and made recommendations for next steps that the Republic could take.<ref name="Recon">{{JournalCite|2|Recon & Report: The Journey to Coruscant}}</ref>

These activities drew the attention of forces within the Empire. [[Bregius Golthan]], one of the group of former [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisors]] to [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] trying to hold the Empire together, was assigned the task of eliminating the Curs and preventing their information from getting back to the New Republic. The renegade [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Utoxx Prentioch]] also learned about the Curs' efforts and believed they could be useful in his fight against Coruscant.<ref name="Blues" /> 

After Harkness's report was compiled but before it was delivered, the Curs, flying in [[Platt Okeefe/Legends|Platt Okeefe's]] freighter ''[[Last Chance (YT-1300 light freighter)/Legends|Last Chance]]'', went to the [[Agriworld/Legends|agriworld]] of [[Moorja/Legends|Moorja]] to pick up a stashed [[BTL-S3 Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing starfighter]] and some other supplies. Golthan tracked them there and sent a fleet to ambush them. Harkness sent an emergency message to General Cracken to ask for help. Before a New Republic team could arrive, a second Imperial fleet from [[Prentioch's Dominion]] arrived and began attacking Golthan's ships.<ref name="Blues" /> 

[[File:Bruwose.png|thumb|left|A bruwose corners Harkness.]]
Caught between the guns of two rival fleets, the ''Last Chance'' landed on the planet in the middle of an [[Ion storm/Legends|ion storm]], where the Curs awaited the arrival of Cracken's relief team. Harkness grew impatient, ignored his companions' advice, and set off on foot in the direction of his Y-wing fighter, carrying the precious data file with him. The fighter was in an underground cave in Moorja's [[Crumbling Lands]], and on his way Harkness found himself cornered by three predatory [[bruwose]]s. Cracken's operatives found him there and helped him get free; Harkness's fighter and the New Republic ship ''[[Valiant Damsel]]'' were then able to help the ''Last Chance'' escape Moorja and deliver the Curs' intelligence to the New Republic fleet.<ref name="Blues" />

===Crystal intrigue===
Some time after the delivery of the report, Cracken tasked the Curs with a mission to the planet [[Canyon]], where the administrator of a mining colony had offered to arrange the sale of strategic minerals to the New Republic. The administrator, [[Guldus Bemm]], also claimed to have found a possible deposit of [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystals]] and had invited [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to come inspect them. Platt Okeefe went alone to manage the negotiation, while the crew of the ''Valiant Damsel'' were chosen to represent Skywalker. Bemm's offer,  however, was a trap. Though disappointed that he could not capture Skywalker, he took the opportunity to capture Okeefe and deliver her to Advisor Golthan. The ''Damsel'' crew escaped, but Okeefe was taken to [[Golthan's Fortress]] on the planet [[Voktunma]].<ref name="Blues" />

[[File:Okeefe is rescued.jpg|thumb|right|The Republic team rescues Okeefe.]]
Mon Mothma expressly forbade General Cracken from committing any Republic resources toward recovering Okeefe, but Harkness recruited the ''Damsel'' crew, who had worked so closely with the Black Curs, to mount a rescue mission on their own without the general's permission. He brought the rogue operatives to [[Black Curs Base]], careful to keep its location a secret from them. Then he and [[Tru'eb Cholakk]] flew the team to Voktunma in Cholakk's freighter, the ''[[Luudrian Star]],'' while other members of the Curs followed in [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]]. The Republic operatives first infiltrated the fortress from the ground and freed Okeefe from her confinement. The ''Luudrian Star'' and the fighters then swooped in, retrieved the team, and escaped the planet.<ref name="Blues" /> 

===Rescue by Rogue Squadron===
In [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]],<ref name="Rogue date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''X-Wing: Rogue Squadron'' to [[6 ABY/Legends|6.5 ABY]].</ref> the Black Curs continued to be employed in scouting operations to some of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core systems]] to assess the strength of the Empire's defensive forces and judge the local political climate: a prelude to the coming [[Core Campaign]]. The Republic provided the team with the captured and modified [[Imperial Customs Frigate]] ''[[Battle of Yavin (starship)|Battle of Yavin]]'' for these missions. While returning to their [[Black Curs Base|hidden base]], the spacers ran into the Imperial [[Strike-class medium cruiser|Strike cruiser]] [[Havoc (Strike-class)|''Havoc'']]. In their attempt to evade the cruiser, they crash-landed the ''Battle of Yavin'' in a lake on the planet [[Hensara III]]. The Republic sent two ships, escorted by the newly-reorganized [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], to rescue the Black Curs and retrieve the ''Battle of Yavin''. The Rogues' extraordinary performance against the ''Havoc''{{'s}} [[TIE series/Legends|starfighter]] complement came to be known as the [[Rout of Hensara]]. The Curs were rescued, though the frigate could not be salvaged and remained at the bottom of the lake.<ref name="Rogue" />

===The ''Vengeance'' Strike===
[[File:Gorkins Rest.jpg|thumb|left|The Curs gather at Gorkin's Rest.]]
Bregius Golthan remained the group's nemesis. In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="SWAJ date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of the Black Curs stories of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].</ref> his personal [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], the ''[[Vengeance (Golthan)|Vengeance]]'', came to [[Wroona Stardock]] for repairs after a costly engagement against New Republic forces. The Imperial Advisor was preparing to launch an attack on [[Sluis Van/Legends|Sluis Van]]. The Black Curs saw their opportunity and launched a mission to sabotage it.<ref name="Vengeance">{{JournalCite|5|Vengeance Strike}}</ref> They gathered at [[Gorkin's Rest]], a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] in [[Wroona/Legends|Wroona]] starport district.<ref name="Explosive">{{JournalCite|3|Explosive Developments}}</ref> Their manner was easygoing, and they spent some time swapping stories of past adventures.<ref name="Quince">{{JournalCite|2|Big Quince (short story)}}</ref> They set out to obtain a supply of [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]] from Harkness's contact at [[Starship Outfitters]],<ref name="Explosive" /> then spent the night aboard Okeefe's ''Last Chance''.<ref name="Starter">{{JournalCite|4|Starter's Tale}}</ref> 

The following day, Harkness and Raventhorn donned the uniforms and fake [[ident badge]]s of Wroona starport technicians. They boarded a shuttle filled with techs bound for the Stardock and boarded the ''Vengeance''. Harkness [[Transmitter/Legends|transmitted]] a signal that overloaded the main [[tracking sensor]] on the ship's bridge. As their fake badges identified them as sensor experts, the two Curs were ordered to go to the bridge to investigate the damage. Officers guided them into one of the [[Data pit|crew pits]], and while Harkness removed the access panel, Raventhorn crawled deep inside the sensor housing and planted their explosive charges against the [[conning tower]]'s outer [[Hull/Legends|bulkhead]].<ref name="Vengeance" />

[[File:Vengeance Golthan.jpg|thumb|right|The ''Vengeance'' at Wroona Stardock]]
Meanwhile, Okeefe, Cholak, and [[Starter]] boarded their ships and left Wroona's atmosphere. Okeefe, in the ''Last Chance'', headed for the star destroyer's landing bay to recover the two saboteurs. Cholak and Starter flew in holding patterns not too far away, waiting to inflict additional damage on the destroyer and, if necessary, help Harkness and Raventhorn escape. However, the getaway was not to be a clean one. Unknown to the Black Curs, Golthan had just contracted the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Beylyssa]]. She knew Raventhorn well, and Golthan wanted her to eliminate the Black Curs before the start of his new campaign. As it happened, Golthan and Beylyssa entered the bridge just as Harkness and Raventhorn were about to make their exit. Golthan recognized them and ordered nearby [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] to attack.<ref name="Vengeance" />

In the ensuing firefight, Raventhorn sprayed the bridge with blaster fire, enough to cover their escape. But they were again delayed, this time by Harkness's own desire for revenge. He moved in to cut out Golthan's eye, repaying the would that the Advisor had inflicted on him under torture. This allowed Beylyssa to regain her footing and shoot back, gravely wounding Harkness in the shoulder and gut. Raventhorn shot back wildly, killing Golthan and wounding Beylyssa enough that she could finally pull her comrade into the relative safety of Golthan's adjoining chamber. The chamber had a rear hatch that led to an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]]. They boarded just as their explosives ripped through the bridge of the ''Vengeance''.<ref name="Vengeance" />

When the blast went off, Starter and Cholak flew in to deal additional damage to the defenseless vessel. In the landing bay, Okeefe waited five more minutes for her friends before taking off, assuming that they had died in their own explosion. Meanwhile the escape pod landed in an ocean on the planet's surface and drifted until waves lodged it in a coastal cave. Freelance salvagers combed the ocean, knowing that the Wroonian [[Salvage Guild]] would pay good money for an Imperial escape pod. A group found Harkness and Raventhorn in the cave in dire need of medical attention. They brought the pair safely to Wroona Starport. The Black Curs' sabotage mission had been enough to damage the ''Vengeance'' beyond repair.<ref name="Vengeance" />

==Members==
*[[Dirk Harkness]]: a dour-faced former starship mechanic, outlaw, and intelligence operative; the founder of the Black Curs and their nominal leader.<ref name="Chessa" />
*[[Platt Okeefe/Legends|Platt Okeefe]]: a notorious smuggler and captain of the ''[[Last Chance (YT-1300 light freighter)/Legends|Last Chance]],'' best known as a guide to promising spacers and the author of several famous handbooks on smuggling.<ref name="Log">{{JournalCite|1|Smuggler's Log}}</ref>
*[[Tru'eb Cholakk]]: Okeefe's longtime companion and protégé and an experienced gunrunner in his own right; owner of the ''[[Luudrian Star]]''.<ref name="Quince" />
*[[Jai Raventhorn]]: a former Rebel Alliance commando and owner of the freighter ''[[Doomsayer]]''.<ref name="Explosive" />
*[[Starter]]: a New Republic fighter pilot who became involved with the Black Curs somewhat later than the others, he flew a modified [[T-65B X-wing starfighter]] and was best known for his wildly improbable stories.<ref name="Starter" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Black Curs first appeared in a series of five stories in the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'', beginning with "[[Chessa's Doom]]" in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|Issue 1]], published in [[February]] [[1994]]. The stories combine narrative prose, written by [[Peter M. Schweighofer]], with short, wordless comics, illustrated by [[John Paul Lona]]. The five interconnected stories all take place on [[Wroona/Legends|Wroona]], where the five core members of the group exchange stories of past adventures. Each installment focuses on one Black Cur, whose story is told via flashback.<ref name="Explosive" />
